subject: Project Management
title: The Spotify Model
description: A 60-second lesson on The Spotify Model.

hook.text: In 2012, Spotify published how it organized engineering: squads, tribes, chapters, and guilds.

buildup.text: Squads were autonomous teams. Tribes grouped squads. Chapters and guilds shared knowledge.

discovery.text: The model spread like wildfire — companies worldwide reorganized into squads and tribes.

twist.text: Spotify itself later said the model was aspirational, not reality. They'd moved on.

climax.text: The industry adopted an idealized snapshot of one company — a model even its creator outgrew.

punchline.text: Everyone copied Spotify. Spotify stopped doing it.

quiz.question: What was ironic about the Spotify model's adoption?
options: Spotify itself said it was aspirational and had moved on
Spotify sued companies for copying it
It only worked for music companies
